documents.title: Model gateway governance
documents.content: LiteLLM is the unified model gateway. Routing decisions should be expressed as task-class to provider/model mappings and not embedded as business literals in orchestration code.

documents.title: Workflow orchestration governance
documents.content: LangGraph orchestration should be decomposed into subgraphs. The runtime uses intent_router_graph, rag_qa_graph, expense_record_graph, schedule_graph, document_summary_graph, external_search_graph, and human_review_graph rather than one monolithic graph.

documents.title: Three-layer state model
documents.content: Execution state is partitioned into session state, user long-term state, and task execution state. Human review checkpoints are stored in task execution state and may suspend the graph until resumed.

documents.title: Retrieval backends
documents.content: Vector retrieval may use Weaviate, Qdrant, or pgvector/PostgreSQL. Document retrieval may use PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, or filesystem metadata. Graph retrieval may use Neo4j or an in-memory graph adapter for local execution.

documents.title: Fallback governance
documents.content: External information access is isolated into external_search_graph. Nodes in other graphs declare a fallback intent but do not run web access directly. Human review may precede or follow fallback when uncertainty remains.
